claim_boundary: The route proves only the declared UI states, tasks, paths, and current observed/implementation evidence; it does not certify unvisited behavior, future AI behavior, or visual design quality.

closure_rules.scope: current target revision and declared route only

forbidden_shortcuts.summary: Do not create a SkillGuard-owned alternate execution route.
Do not convert configured booleans, generated JSON, or progress into native pass evidence.
Do not skip required stages, checks, receipts, or blockers.

integration_claim_boundary: SkillGuard gates the target-native FlowGuard route; it does not execute a parallel controller or replace native model/test evidence.

native_check_bindings.evidence_source: current model runner receipt and final report

native_check_inventory.summary: Run UI flow, visible-surface, operability, and implementation evidence model checks.

native_route_bindings.source: FlowGuard canonical route registry and target contract source

not_parallel_route_proof.evidence_source: Canonical route registry, contract source, and may_define_* false gates
not_parallel_route_proof.summary: SkillGuard only validates the FlowGuard-owned route and cannot define an alternate successful execution path.

phases.summary: Declare greenfield, source_based, or mixed mode; product/user scope; source authority; and requested UI claim.
Inventory observed visible items, required capabilities/outputs, tasks, controls, displays, states, events, source mappings, and blindspots.
Build interaction/journey/structure/text/task contracts and current functional chains, implementation runs, geometry, and transition targets as required.
Run the UI Flow model and reject unowned enabled actions, missing recovery, stale/weak evidence, or design-only runnable claims.
Return current UI evidence and gaps; hand semantic transition cells and large evidence matrices to MTA/TestMesh.

quality_floors.summary: Prompt prose, progress, or generated records cannot replace native evidence.
Missing, stale, skipped, scoped, progress-only, or pass-with-gaps evidence cannot support broad closure.

required_evidence.source: FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact

route_inventory.summary: Model UI behavior and prove observed surface, capability/task coverage, journeys, operability, implementation evidence, geometry, and transition scope.

do_not_use_when: The work is visual styling only, non-UI, code-structure planning, or prose/label/API evidence is being substituted for UI behavior proof.
routes.summary: Model UI behavior and prove observed surface, capability/task coverage, journeys, operability, implementation evidence, geometry, and transition scope.

runtime_lock_policy.applies_when: The flowguard-ui-flow-structure skill is selected or delegated.
runtime_lock_policy.summary: The target-local contract, route, stage order, checks, evidence, and coverage matrix gate closure.

source_requirements.summary: Accept greenfield, source_based, or mixed scope; inventory surface/capabilities; block unowned enabled actions, missing recovery, or design-only runnable claims; hand transitions to MTA/Tes.
Route id: ui_flow_structure; role: public_owner; native owner: ui_flow_structure. This standalone FlowGuard satellite skill owns UI behavior/surface evidence, not visual styling or code str.
Use for UI controls, displays, dialogs, menus, navigation, visible surface, tasks, blindspots, reachable enabled action chains, implementation runs, or layout/responsiveness evidence.
The target section 'Use When' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Every reachable enabled action needs control -> event -> owner -> function -> UI update -> click/test evidence, pure-UI disposition, or owned blindspot.
The target section 'Hard Gates' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Return evidence, failures, blockers, skipped_checks, residual_risk, claim_boundary, and typed_next_actions, plus a UI state diagram, task/control coverage, and blindspots.
Verify the real FlowGuard check engine and AGENTS.md managed record; never create a fake mini-framework.
The target skill description must be represented in the contract: Use for UI behavior, observed surface, capabilities, interaction states, journeys, structure/text, human operability, runna.
The target skill entrypoint and hard gates must be represented by the runtime contract.
description: Use for UI behavior, observed surface, capabilities, interaction states, journeys, structure/text, human operability, runnable evidence, geometry, responsiveness, or transition.
Declared hard gates and required workflow steps must be represented by checks and closure blockers.
Do not style first, prose-validate, treat labels/API routes as functional proof, or replace frontend/design/code-structure workflows; return non-UI work to model-first-function-flow.
The target section 'Do Not Use When' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
The target section 'Output Requirements' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Build UI states/events/controls/displays, journey coverage, structure, text hierarchy, task coverage, and human-operability evidence.
Declare work mode; inventory observed surface and required capabilities when a UI exists or runs.
Design evidence cannot prove runnable UI; source/mixed work needs baseline alignment, failures/recovery stay explicit, and deepened UI models require template harvest closure.
Edit .skillguard/contract-source.json, then regenerate derived contracts; SkillGuard checks the UI route contract and cannot fabricate click-through, screenshot, DOM, or human evidence.
For runnable claims, bind screenshot/DOM/event/result evidence, click chains, geometry/transitions, blindspots, and tests.
Model UI event x UI state behavior and prove the declared boundary from observed surface through runnable evidence.
The target section 'Required Workflow' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Model/design evidence cannot prove implemented/runnable completion; source alignment, recovery, human, and current evidence-kind gates remain explicit.
Every reachable enabled action has a modeled event and current functional chain/click evidence, pure-UI disposition, or owned blindspot.
Existing/runnable UI claims require complete observed-surface and user-visible capability/output accounting.
